questions.question: What is the slope of the line $y = 2x + 1$?
A basketball player scores $y$ points in $x$ games. If $y = 3x - 2$, what is the rate of change of points scored per game?
The table shows the distance $d$ traveled by a cyclist in $t$ hours. What is the slope of the line?
The line $y = -2x + 5$ represents the score of a tennis player. What is the slope of the line?
The graph shows the height $h$ of a basketball player after $t$ years. What is the slope of the line?
What is the slope of a vertical line?
The equation $y = 0.5x + 2$ represents the distance $y$ traveled by a runner in $x$ minutes. What is the rate of change of distance traveled per minute?
A football player throws a ball with an initial velocity of $v$ m/s. The equation $d = vt$ represents the distance $d$ traveled by the ball in $t$ seconds. What is the slope of the line?

The table shows the number of points $y$ scored by a team in $x$ games. Calculate the slope of the line and interpret it as the rate of change of points scored per game. Show your work and explain your answer.

The equation $y = -3x + 10$ represents the number of yards $y$ a football team has to go to score a touchdown in $x$ plays. Calculate the slope of the line and interpret it as the rate of change of yards to go per play. Show your work and explain your answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of slope and rate of change.
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of interpreting slope in the context of real-world problems.
Tip 3: Encourage students to show their work and explain their answers in the free-response questions.
